title: Sample Preparation for 3D-Raman Microspectroscopic Mapping of Protist Cells
author: Felix Weber
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ztyf6pw

steps.instruction: Centrifugation of formaldehyde-fixed sample at 150 rcf for 10 min.
Resuspend cell suspension with Volvic spring water by repipetting or gentle vortexing.
Place cleaned stainless steel square in a petri dish.
Fill petri dish with Volvic water and place it on the microscope stage.
Keep washed cell suspension on ice to increase water density. This enhances the sample’s sinking properties and improves cell adhesion to the stainless steel slide while loading.
Retrieve a 10-40 µl subsample from the cell suspension with a 100 µl micropipette.
Insert pipette tip into petri dish until it touches the stainless steel slide.

Place two beakers with water-saturated blotting paper in the microspectrometer's dome to maintain 100% relative humidity and minimize evaporative losses from the petri dish during extended Raman data acquisition runs.

Start Raman interrogation of the whole cell of interest within a 3-D grid set at the desired spatial resolution. Further information on the workflow for Raman microspectroscopic mapping of macromolecules in fully hydrated protist cells can be found in Taylor GT (2019) Windows into Microbial Seascapes: Advances in Nanoscale Imaging and Application to Marine Sciences. Ann Rev Mar Sci 11:annurev-marine-121916-063612
Delicately load sample onto the stainless steel slide while minimizing agitation.
Gently insert the water immersion 63x objective lens into the water until specimens on the stainless steel slide are visible in the microscope.
Repeat steps 1-3 three times and keep cell suspension at 4°C.

3-D picture and video animation of intracellular protein (cyan) and DNA (red) distribution in axenically grown Tetrahymena pyriformis cells. Picture: Video (available in online version): Raman volume map of spectral peak intensities showing subcellular nucleic acid (red, wavenumber position 784 cm -1 ) and protein (cyan, wavenumber position 1002 cm -1 ; phenylalanine used as a protein proxy) distributions in a fully-hydrated, preserved Tetrahymena cell. Subcellular structures such as micro- and macro- nuclei (red) are resolved. Data set was acquired during a 13 hour Raman scan with a 633 nm He/Ne laser along a 3-D grid with a spatial resolution of 2 µm x 2 µm x 4 µm in XYZ dimensions, yielding 3472 individual spectra.
